title: Lessons from a project that finished 3 months late
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to harvest, analyze root causes, and produce actionable recommendations from a delayed project.
input: Project Alpha was a 12-month ERP implementation that delivered 3 months late and 20% over budget. Key events: requirements changed 4 times after sign-off, the lead architect resigned at month 6, integration testing was compressed from 8 weeks to 3 weeks, and the client escalated twice to the steering committee. The team ran 4 sprint retrospectives and 1 post-mortem. Stakeholder satisfaction scored 2.8/5. Produce a full lessons learned register with root cause analysis and actionable recommendations.
expected_behavior: Harvests lessons from all listed sources: retrospectives, post-mortem, stakeholder satisfaction, escalation records, and schedule/budget variances
Identifies at least 3 distinct root causes using 5 Whys or fishbone (e.g., scope creep governance failure, single-point-of-failure in architecture staffing, compressed testing window)
Classifies lessons by project phase and domain (scope, schedule, cost, quality, risk, stakeholder)
Produces actionable recommendations with owner, priority, and implementation steps
Distinguishes between symptoms (late delivery) and root causes (change control process gaps)

title: Capturing positive lessons from a highly successful agile delivery
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to capture and codify success factors — not just failures — from a high-performing agile team.
input: Project Beta was a 6-month mobile app delivery using Scrum with 2-week sprints. It delivered 2 weeks early, under budget, with a stakeholder satisfaction score of 4.7/5. The team had stable velocity after sprint 3, zero critical defects in production, and the client praised the demo cadence. The PO was embedded full-time and the team practiced mob programming for complex features. Capture the positive lessons learned and package them as replicable practices for future agile projects.
expected_behavior: Extracts positive lessons with the same rigor as negative ones — not just 'everything went well'
Identifies specific success drivers: embedded PO, mob programming, stable velocity, demo cadence
Applies root cause analysis to successes — explains WHY these practices produced the outcome
Packages replicable practices with conditions and context (team size, domain, methodology) so future teams know when they apply
Produces recommendations that are adoption-oriented: how to introduce these practices in teams that don't currently use them

title: Cross-project pattern analysis from a portfolio of 5 completed projects
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to detect systemic patterns across multiple projects rather than treating each in isolation.
input: Analyze lessons from 5 completed projects in the 2025 portfolio: (1) Project Alpha — ERP, 3 months late, scope creep issues; (2) Project Beta — Mobile app, early delivery, strong PO engagement; (3) Project Gamma — Data migration, on time but 3 critical production defects, testing was outsourced; (4) Project Delta — Cloud migration, 6 weeks late, vendor dependency delays; (5) Project Epsilon — API platform, on time and budget, but team burnout reported at 70% by closure. Identify cross-project patterns, systemic issues, and organizational-level recommendations.
expected_behavior: Detects recurring patterns across the 5 projects rather than analyzing each in isolation
Identifies at least 2 systemic patterns (e.g., testing quality issues appear in Alpha and Gamma; schedule pressure appears in Alpha, Delta, and Epsilon)
Distinguishes project-specific lessons from organizational-level patterns
Maps patterns to organizational dimensions: methodology, team structure, vendor management, quality assurance
Produces portfolio-level recommendations targeting the PMO or organizational leadership, not just individual project teams

title: Mid-project lessons capture during a sprint retrospective
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to capture lessons in-flight rather than waiting for project closure, enabling real-time course correction.
input: We are at sprint 8 of a 20-sprint project (Project Zeta, healthcare platform). The last 3 retrospectives flagged: (1) environment provisioning takes 3-5 days and blocks every sprint; (2) the BA and QA are not aligned on acceptance criteria — 40% of stories get bounced back; (3) the client stakeholder misses 50% of refinement sessions causing last-minute scope changes. We have not yet done a formal lessons capture. Produce a mid-project lessons register with immediate corrective actions — do not wait for closure.
expected_behavior: Captures lessons immediately from retrospective data without requiring project closure
Treats in-flight lessons with urgency — recommendations are corrective actions for the current project, not just future reference
Applies root cause analysis to the 3 flagged issues (environment provisioning, BA-QA misalignment, stakeholder absence)
Produces time-bound corrective actions with owners that can be implemented in the next 1-2 sprints
Distinguishes between quick wins (implement now) and structural changes (plan for next quarter) in the recommendations
